data.title: CloudTalk: The Cloud Call Center Built for Growth
data.excerpt: Discover CloudTalk, the cloud-based call center platform with AI analytics, smart IVR, and 160+ country numbers. See if it fits your team.

$media.alt: CloudTalk: The Cloud Call Center Built for Growth

children.text: *Disclosure: This post contains affiliate links. If you sign up through them, we may earn a commission at no extra cost to you.*

If your sales or support team relies on phone calls to close deals and keep customers happy, you already know the pain: dropped calls, messy data, no visibility into what's actually happening on the line. CloudTalk solves that—cleanly, affordably, and without forcing you to rip out your existing workflow.

I've spent time digging into this platform, reading through hundreds of user reviews, testing features, and comparing how it stacks up in real-world scenarios. Here's everything you need to know about CloudTalk and why it might be the call center upgrade your team has been waiting for.
Check out CloudTalk's pricing and plans here
What Exactly Is CloudTalk?

CloudTalk is a cloud-based phone system designed specifically for sales teams and customer support departments. Instead of relying on clunky on-premise hardware or stitching together five different tools, CloudTalk gives you a single platform where your team can make and receive calls, track performance, and automate the boring stuff.

The platform serves over 2,500 companies worldwide. It connects to your existing CRM—whether that's HubSpot, Salesforce, Pipedrive, or dozens of others—so call data flows directly into your pipeline without anyone lifting a finger.

What makes it stand out right away is the reach: you get local and toll-free numbers in more than 160 countries. That means if your team in Austin needs to call a prospect in Berlin, they show up with a German number on the caller ID. Pick-up rates go up. Conversations happen. Deals move forward.
How CloudTalk Works in Practice

Setting up CloudTalk doesn't require an IT department. You sign up, pick your numbers, connect your CRM, and you're making calls within the hour. The interface is browser-based, so there's nothing to install—agents log in from anywhere and start dialing.
Here's a concrete example of what a typical workflow looks like:
Inbound call arrives.
CloudTalk's smart IVR (Interactive Voice Response) greets the caller and routes them based on language, department, or custom rules you set.
The right agent picks up.
Skills-based routing makes sure the call goes to someone qualified to handle it, not just whoever is free.
Call gets recorded automatically.
Every conversation is logged, tagged, and stored for training, compliance, or dispute resolution.
AI analytics kick in.
CloudTalk transcribes the call, scores sentiment, and flags coaching opportunities—all without a manager listening to every recording manually.
The entire cycle feeds back into your CRM. A sales rep hanging up the phone sees the call log, notes, and next steps already populated in their deal pipeline. Zero double-entry.
If you're already using a project management tool like ClickUp, you can integrate it too—
here's our guide on how to use ClickUp alongside tools like CloudTalk
Key Features That Teams Actually Use
Let me walk through the features that come up most often in user reviews and that genuinely move the needle for teams.
Smart Call Routing and IVR

CloudTalk's IVR isn't the robotic "press 1 for sales" experience you dread. You can build multi-level menus, set business hours, create overflow rules, and even offer callback queues so customers don't sit on hold. The drag-and-drop builder means you can redesign your call flow on a Tuesday afternoon without calling your developer.
Call Recording and Transcription

Every call can be recorded and automatically transcribed. Managers search transcripts by keyword—say, a competitor's name or a pricing objection—and pull up the exact moment in seconds. For training new reps, this is gold. You don't have to sit through 45-minute calls hoping to find a teachable moment.
Real-Time and Historical Analytics

The dashboard shows live metrics: who's on a call, average wait time, abandonment rate, and agent availability. Zoom out to the historical view and you get trends over weeks or months—perfect for forecasting staffing needs or identifying which campaigns drive the most inbound volume.
Power Dialer

For outbound-heavy teams, CloudTalk's power dialer automates the tedious part. It pulls numbers from your contact list, dials sequentially, skips voicemails, and connects agents only when a real person answers. Reps spend time talking instead of punching digits.
CRM Integrations

CloudTalk plugs into over 35 CRMs and business tools natively. The Salesforce and HubSpot integrations are particularly deep—you get screen pops with caller info, automatic logging, and click-to-call directly from your pipeline. If you're building out your sales stack,
our Apollo.io guide
pairs well with CloudTalk for prospecting and outreach.
Start your free CloudTalk trial
New and Highlighted Features
CloudTalk ships updates frequently, and a few recent additions deserve attention.
AI-Powered Conversation Intelligence

The platform's AI engine now scores calls automatically based on talk-to-listen ratio, sentiment, keywords mentioned, and follow-up actions agreed upon. Managers get a dashboard showing which reps need coaching and which calls led to positive outcomes. It's like having a QA team working 24/7 at a fraction of the cost.
SMS and MMS Messaging

CloudTalk expanded beyond voice. Agents can now send and receive text messages from the same interface, using the same business numbers. For appointment confirmations, quick follow-ups, or sharing links, SMS keeps the conversation moving without the formality of another call.
Click-to-Call Browser Extension

The Chrome extension lets agents highlight any phone number on a webpage and call it instantly. Sales reps prospecting on LinkedIn, Crunchbase, or company websites save a surprising amount of time with this tiny feature.
Enhanced Workflow Automations

You can now build if-then rules directly inside CloudTalk: if a call goes unanswered three times, send an automatic SMS; if a deal reaches a certain stage, trigger a follow-up call task. These micro-automations compound into hours saved each week.
For a complete breakdown of what's new,
check our CloudTalk new features page
Who Is CloudTalk Ideal For?
CloudTalk isn't trying to be everything to everyone. It shines brightest for specific teams and use cases.
Sales teams doing outbound prospecting.

The power dialer, local presence numbers, and CRM integrations make high-volume outbound calling feel organized rather than chaotic. If your reps make 50–200 calls a day, this setup respects their time.
Customer support teams handling inbound volume.
Smart IVR, skills-based routing, and queue management keep wait times down and first-call resolution rates up. The analytics help managers staff correctly during peak hours.
Remote and distributed teams.

Because it's entirely cloud-based, agents work from anywhere with an internet connection. No VPN, no desk phones shipped across continents. Onboarding a new rep in Lisbon takes the same effort as onboarding one in your headquarters.
Growing companies scaling internationally.
Getting phone numbers in 160+ countries without negotiating with local telecoms is a massive time-saver. CloudTalk handles the complexity behind the scenes.
Agencies and consultancies managing multiple clients.
If you run support or sales operations for several brands, CloudTalk's workspace structure lets you segment call flows, analytics, and numbers by client cleanly.
Teams already using monday.com for project coordination will find the integration smooth—
here's our monday.com tutorial
that shows how to connect the two.
For deeper use case examples,
visit our CloudTalk use cases page
Pricing and Plans
CloudTalk offers three main tiers: Starter, Essential, and Expert. Pricing scales with features rather than per-minute charges, which means your bill stays predictable even as call volume grows.

The Starter plan covers the basics—unlimited inbound calls, IVR, call recording, and integrations. The Essential tier adds the power dialer, advanced analytics, and more automation options. Expert unlocks AI conversation intelligence, custom reporting, and priority support.
Every plan comes with a free trial, so you can test the platform with your actual team before committing. No contracts, no setup fees.
See CloudTalk's latest pricing and start your trial
What Real Users Say
CloudTalk holds a 4.4 out of 5 rating on G2 with nearly 1,900 reviews. That's not a fluke—it reflects consistent satisfaction across company sizes.

Users frequently mention three things: the intuitive interface that gets new agents productive within a day, the quality of customer support when issues arise, and the depth of CRM integrations that eliminate manual data entry.

The few criticisms tend to focus on feature requests—teams wanting even more customization in reporting or additional native integrations. But CloudTalk's release cadence addresses these steadily, which users acknowledge.
If you want practical advice on getting the most from the platform,
our CloudTalk tips guide
covers best practices from power users.
Why CloudTalk Over Alternatives
The cloud call center market has plenty of options. Aircall, RingCentral, Dialpad, and others all compete for your attention. Here's where CloudTalk carves its niche.

It's purpose-built for sales and support teams, not general business telephony. Every feature—from the power dialer to AI call scoring—serves that mission directly. You're not paying for video conferencing extras or internal chat tools you already have elsewhere.

The pricing is transparent and competitive. Where some platforms charge per minute on top of subscription fees, CloudTalk keeps it simple with per-user pricing that includes calling to major destinations.

And the integration depth matters. CloudTalk doesn't just "connect" to your CRM—it syncs call data bidirectionally, meaning changes in either platform reflect instantly. For teams that live inside their CRM, this tight coupling saves hours every week.
For a full comparison of why teams choose CloudTalk,
read our detailed guide
Getting Started
If any of this sounds like what your team needs, the path forward is simple:
1. Visit
CloudTalk's website
and sign up for a free trial.
2. Pick your numbers—local, toll-free, or international.
3. Connect your CRM and import contacts.
4. Set up your IVR and call routing rules.
5. Invite your team and start making calls.
Most teams are fully operational within a single afternoon. The learning curve is minimal, and CloudTalk's onboarding resources guide you through each step.
For a step-by-step walkthrough,
our CloudTalk how-to guide
covers everything from first login to advanced configuration.

children.text: What is CloudTalk and what does it do?

CloudTalk is a cloud-based call center platform for sales and support teams. It provides phone numbers in 160+ countries, smart IVR routing, call recording, AI-powered analytics, and deep CRM integrations—all accessible from a browser without hardware.
How much does CloudTalk cost?

CloudTalk offers tiered plans starting with the Starter package for essential features, scaling up to Essential and Expert tiers with advanced analytics, power dialer, and AI conversation intelligence. All plans include unlimited inbound calls and a free trial with no setup fees.
Does CloudTalk integrate with Salesforce and HubSpot?

Yes. CloudTalk has native, bidirectional integrations with Salesforce, HubSpot, Pipedrive, and over 35 other CRMs. Call data syncs automatically—logs, recordings, and notes appear in your CRM without manual entry.
Is CloudTalk good for remote teams?

Absolutely. CloudTalk is entirely browser-based, so agents can work from anywhere with an internet connection. There's no hardware to ship, no VPN required, and onboarding a new remote rep takes minutes.
